name: route-guard
title: Functional route guard composition
description: Pattern for authoring functional CanActivateFn guards and composing them on route declarations. Cena uses functional guards (not class-based CanActivate). Pure pattern entry — no component source.

cena.whenToUse: Whenever a route needs auth, role, onboarding-state, or feature-flag gating. Multiple guards compose by listing them in canActivate array; all must return true for navigation to proceed.

templates.routeComposition: { path: 'x', loadComponent: () => import('./x.component').then(m => m.XComponent), canActivate: [authGuard, onboardingGuard] }

parameters.Service: Angular service providing the gating logic
parameters.check: Method on the service returning the boolean
parameters.fallback: Route path to redirect to when the gate fails

gotchas: Use inject() inside the functional guard body, not constructor — functional guards have no class context
Return UrlTree (via Router.createUrlTree) instead of returning false bare — provides clean redirect semantics with explicit destination
Avoid stateful checks inside guards; delegate to services that own the state
Functional guards execute in array order; place cheapest checks first (e.g., auth before role lookup that requires a server call)

preferenceLedgerRows.name: functional DI via inject()
preferenceLedgerRows.applies: guards use inject() inside the functional body

preferenceLedgerRows.name: Angular 21.2.13
preferenceLedgerRows.applies: functional guards are Angular 14.2+; we are on 21.x

references: Andrey's anchor: cena-health-spark/patients/src/app/guards/ (4 functional guards — reference for our shape)
